commit.message:
feat: do not forward manael transform query params to upstream (#1651)

- [x] Strip Manael transform query params from outbound upstream URL in
`resizeProxy.ServeHTTP`
- [x] Fix: only strip resize params (`w`, `h`, `fit`) when
`EnableResize` is true; always strip `q` (quality is always consumed)
- [x] Add subtest verifying resize params are forwarded when resize is
disabled
- [x] Revert accidental changes to `website/go.mod` and `website/go.sum`
- [x] All existing tests pass

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Do not forward Manael transform query params to
upstream</issue_title>
> <issue_description>## Summary
> Requests with Manael transform parameters are currently forwarded to
upstream as-is.
> 
> `w`, `h`, `fit` (and similarly transform-only params such as `q`) are
parsed by Manael, but remain on the outbound upstream URL.
> 
> ## Current behavior
> - `ServeHTTP` reads query params for transform options.
> - Request is then proxied without removing transform-only params.
> - Upstream receives those params.
> 
> ## Why this is a problem
> - Leaks internal transform controls to origin.
> - Can create unnecessary cache key fragmentation at origin/CDN.
> - May trigger unintended behavior if origin uses same param names.
> 
> ## Expected behavior
> Manael-specific transform parameters should be consumed by Manael and
**not** forwarded upstream.
> Non-Manael query params should continue to be forwarded unchanged.
> 
> ## Reproduction
> 1. Run Manael with any upstream that can echo request URI/query.
> 2. Request an image via Manael, e.g.:
>    - `/photo.jpeg?w=200&h=200&fit=cover`
> 3. Observe upstream receives `w/h/fit` in query string.
> 
> ## Proposed fix direction
> - Before proxying, clone request URL query and remove transform
params:
>   - resize: `w`, `h`, `fit`
> - quality: `q` (and any format-specific quality keys supported by
Manael)
> - Keep all unrelated query params intact.
> - Add tests that verify upstream does not receive Manael transform
params while preserving unrelated params.
> 
> ## Acceptance criteria
> - Upstream no longer receives Manael transform-only query params.
> - Existing transform behavior remains unchanged.
> - Non-transform query params still pass through.
> - Unit/integration tests cover this behavior.
> </issue_description>
> 
> ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section)
> 
> <comments>
> </comments>
> 


</details>

commit.message:
Fix debug APK package conflict: unconditional signing + applicationIdSuffix (#55)

* Initial plan

* Fix package conflict: remove if-guard from signingConfig, add applicationIdSuffix for debug builds

- Remove the conditional `if (debugKeystorePath.exists())` guard from
  signingConfigs.debug. The guard silently fell back to Android's SDK-generated
  debug keystore (different on each runner VM) when the file wasn't found,
  causing INSTALL_FAILED_UPDATE_INCOMPATIBLE. The committed keystore is now
  always used unconditionally; if it's missing the build fails loudly.

- Add applicationIdSuffix ".debug" to buildTypes.debug, giving debug APKs
  the package ID com.hyliankid14.bbcradioplayer.debug. This prevents conflicts
  when a release build (F-Droid / Play Store) is already installed on the
  same device — they now have different package identifiers so they can
  coexist and be updated independently.

Co-authored-by: hyliankid14 <1757523+hyliankid14@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Agent: Target State Alignment (#7564)

* Agent: Target State Alignment

* Initial plan

* Enforce formatter boundary: remove collapseVertexFormatBeginSpacing from normalize-formatted-output.ts

Removes collapseVertexFormatBeginSpacing and collapseCustomFunctionToFormatEndSpacing
from the formatter's post-processing pipeline. These functions violated
target-state.md §2.1 and §3.2 by hardcoding GML API names
(vertex_format_begin, vertex_format_end) and making content-aware spacing
decisions based on those API names—a semantic/content rewrite that belongs
exclusively in @gml-modules/lint, not the formatter.

Also removes the five helper functions (stripInlineLineComment,
isSimpleFunctionCallLine, isVertexFormatEndAssignmentLine) and the two
regex patterns that only existed to serve the removed functions.

Adds a boundary test in normalize-output-formatter-boundary.test.ts that
asserts the formatter does NOT collapse blank lines around
vertex_format_begin/end calls, locking in the contract so the violation
cannot be silently re-introduced.

commit.message:
refactor: migrate JWT middleware from v2 to v3 (#400)

* feat: migrate go-jwt-middleware from v2 to v3

- Update module dependency from v2.3.1 to v3.0.0
- Migrate JWKS provider to use options pattern (WithIssuerURL, WithCacheTTL, WithCustomClient)
- Migrate validator to use options pattern (WithKeyFunc, WithAlgorithm, WithIssuer, WithAudiences)
- Update claims access pattern: use type assertion on ValidateToken return value
- Update error handling and API signatures for v3 compatibility
- Add issuerURL parameter to NewAuth() since IssuerURL is no longer exported from CachingProvider

Breaking changes in v3:
- Pure options pattern for all constructors (no positional arguments)
- ValidateToken returns (interface{}, error) with type assertion required
- NewCachingProvider now returns error as second value
- All tests pass successfully
